meta.title: Family Gerende Kirigamine Ski Area
meta.description: 360° mountain views at family-friendly prices—the safest first snow day on the Kirigamine plateau.

hero.title:
Family Gerende
Kirigamine
hero.tagline: 360° panoramas at striking family prices—your safest first snow day.

live.subtitle: Suwa City portal (no live snow API)

sanctuary.title:
No steep pitches,
full panoramas.
sanctuary.lead: A highland resort with views of Mt. Fuji and the Japan Alps. Gentle, wide terrain built for beginners, families, and first-timers—not experts chasing steeps.
sanctuary.noticeBody: Weekdays closed in Feb–Mar (weekends/holidays only). Winter road restrictions on Kirigamine and Venus lines—snow tires and route checks required. Official updates via Suwa City portal.
sanctuary.imgAlt: Panorama over the Kirigamine plateau—gentle family terrain

primary.title: Moving walkway × Snow Kids Kirigamine
primary.lead: The 50 m "Rakuchinkun" walkway makes sledding and first turns easy. The 2017 kids park offers snow racers and tubes—sled and ski zones are separated so little ones never cross busy slopes.
primary.imgAlt: Kids park and gentle slopes—family-first design

secondary.title: Kirigamine Ski School
secondary.lead: SAJ-certified school on site—from group lessons for juniors to private coaching, built to lock in basics on a first snow day.

bundle.title:
Industry-low tickets,
family-first.
bundle.lead: Adult day pass ¥2,610; child/junior high & senior (55+) ¥1,570. Preschoolers ski free with a guardian. Walkway-only ¥520; kids park bundle ¥1,010. Third Sunday each month: free lifts for junior high and below.
bundle.imgAlt: Families on gentle Kirigamine terrain—value and peace of mind

wellness.title:
After skiing,
to Lake Suwa.
wellness.lead: 30–40 minutes down to Kamisuwa onsen. Soak in quality hot springs and enjoy lakeside dining—a strong add-on for a 1-night trip, not just a day visit from Tokyo or Nagoya.
wellness.leadStation: Cottages next to the hill let kids build kamakura snow huts—a stay-and-play retreat on the plateau.
wellness.imgAlt: Kamisuwa onsen and Lake Suwa—after-ski wellness

tour.title:
From the city,
snow debut + onsen.
tour.lead: About 30–40 minutes from Chuo Expressway Suwa IC. Realistic as a day trip from greater Tokyo or Chukyo—or pair with lakeside hot springs overnight.

01.title: Suwa IC · Kamisuwa
01.body: Via Kirigamine road · snow tires required

ecosystem.title: Pricing that widens the base
ecosystem.body: Remarkably low lift prices while the industry raises rates. Free preschool lifts, free kids' day on the third Sunday, weekday closures in late season—focused operations that bring new families into snow sports.

access.title: Suwa IC & winter roads
access.body: ~17 km / 30–40 min from Suwa IC. ~30–40 min by car/taxi from JR Kamisuwa Station. Winter restrictions on Venus Line and nearby routes—check conditions and use snow tires.

green.title: Beyond downhill
green.body: Snowshoe and cross-country trails too—gateway to Kirigamine nature including treks toward Mt. Kuruma-yama summit.

access.note: ~17 km from Suwa IC · 30–40 min · JR Kamisuwa ~30–40 min by car/taxi · ~500 free parking spaces
access.phoneBus: Bus from Kamisuwa · check winter timetable
access.rentacarLink: Rent a car near Suwa IC
access.rentacarHint: Watch winter closures on Kirigamine road

footer.copyright: © Family Gerende Kirigamine Ski Area — LP mock

nearbyFood.notice: Hours and menus—check each venue's official site.
nearbyFood.introLead: Kamisuwa and Lake Suwa host beloved local restaurants.
nearbyFood.introBody: From slope-side cafés to lakeside classics—four family-friendly picks.

hokurei.title: Kogen no Mise (on hill)
hokurei.lead: Popular slope-side set meals—ramen combos that families return for.

yuyu.title: Lake Suwa unagi
yuyu.lead: Regional grilled eel—a classic reward after skiing.

pipicafe.title: Kirigamine hand-cut soba
pipicafe.lead: Highland buckwheat noodles—light lunch or dinner.

otokoyama.title: Miyasaka Brewery (Suwa)
otokoyama.lead: Local sake and miso—souvenirs for adults.

nearbyOnsen.introLead: Lakeside baths after skiing—what makes a 1-night itinerary worthwhile.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: Kamisuwa onsen plus lakeside ryokan—a two-base recovery model.

yuyu.title: Kamisuwa onsen (Katakurakan, etc.)
yuyu.lead: One of Suwa's five famous springs—open-air baths overlooking the lake.

kamoi.title: Lakeside onsen ryokan
kamoi.lead: Stay by the lake, return to the hill next morning—a family 1-night pattern.

corridorStay.notice: Rates and rooms—check official booking sites.
corridorStay.introLead: Cottage next to the hill for snow play, or lakeside onsen for a slower pace.

yuyu.title: Kirigamine Cottage
yuyu.lead: Closest stay to the hill—kamakura play and snow fun for kids.

mount-city.title: Kirigamine highland lodge
mount-city.lead: Near the ski area—first tracks for early-rising families.

omo7.title: Katakurakan
omo7.lead: Historic Kamisuwa onsen—maximize a ski + hot spring trip.

dormy.title: Lakeside onsen hotel
dormy.lead: Lake views and Suwa dining—back to the hill next morning.
